query: Adverse events with mRNA vaccines in pediatrics

articles.title: Respiratory syncytial virus mRNA vaccine-induced immunity and protection against subgroups A and B in mice
articles.summary: AbstractRespiratory syncytial virus (RSV) is a major global cause of severe lower respiratory tract infections in infants and older adults. mRNA vaccine encoding prefusion F protein-based vaccines achieved 70% efficacy, and moderna’s new vaccine for older adult populations further underscores the potential of rSV-a and resultaneous titers.

articles.title: Signals of disproportionate reporting of events supposedly attributable to COVID-19 vaccines in children and adolescents: case-non-case study, Brazil, 2022
articles.journal: Epidemiologia e Serviços de Saúde : Revista do Sistema Unico de Saúde do Brasil
articles.summary: in 2022, there were 225,693 reports, of which 26,128 were vaccine-event pairs in children and adolescents, mostly non-serious events. we traced 39 signals of disproportionate reporting for the Sinovac vaccine and 53 signals for Pfizer-BioNTech, including signals not described in the package insert.

articles.title: Hematologic adverse events following COVID-19 vaccination and revaccination in persons aged 12 and above: A Canadian Immunization Research Network study
articles.summary: study characterized hematologic AEFIs in patients assessed by SIC network physicians from January 2021 to February 2023. participants were followed for recurrence after revaccination with an mRNA vaccine, including the two patients with TTS.

articles.title: RSV vaccine development: advances and fusion protein-focused strategies
articles.summary: Respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) is a leading cause of acute lower respiratory tract infections. it can progress to severe pulmonary disease in these vulnerable populations, frequently necessitating hospitalization. this review begins with RSV molecular virology, summarises the evolution of prophylactic antibody, and revisits lessons from past vaccine failures.

articles.title: Safety, immunogenicity, and effectiveness of the MAV/06 varicella vaccine: A comprehensive review of a new strain vaccine
articles.summary: ABSTRACTMost of licensed varicella vaccines are based on the Oka strain. the MAV/06 strain vaccine was exclusively developed by GC Biopharma Corp.
